records.ety: [L. aequivocatus, p. p. of aequivocari to be called by the same name, fr. L. aequivocus: cf. F. quivoquer. See Equivocal, a.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Equivocated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Equivocating.]

defs: To use words of equivocal or doubtful signification; to express one's opinions in terms which admit of different senses, with intent to deceive; to use ambiguous expressions with a view to mislead; as, to equivocate is the work of duplicity.
quotes: All that Garnet had to say for him was that he supposed he meant to equivocate.
He equivocated his vow by a mental reservation.
